title: Gurdwara Handi Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Handi Sahib — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Gurdwara Handi Sahib is situated in Danapur is a cantonment station, 20 km west of old Patna City.
overview.description: Gurdwara Handi Sahib is situated in Danapur is a cantonment station, 20 km west of old Patna City. Guru Tegh Bahadur had returned to Punjab in April 1670 leaving his family behind at Patna. The family after leaving Patna Sahib made their first halt here. An old lady named Jamani Mai served a kettleful (handi) of khichari to them after which the shrine subsequently built here was named as Handiwali Sangat, which is now called Gurdwara Handi Sahib.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Guru ka Bagh
topPlaces.description: About three kilometers east of Takht Sri Patna Sahib is where Guru Tegh Bahadur first alighted in a garden (bagh) belonging to Nawabs Rahim Bakhsh and Karim Bakhsh, nobles of Patna, and where the sangat of Patna along with the young Guru Gobind Singh came out to receive him back from his four-year-long odyssey. A shrine commemorative of the first meeting of Tegh Bahadur and Gobind Singh was established here.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat in a narrow lane close to Takht Sri Harmandir Sahib marks the house where King Fateh Chand Maini lived. His childless Queen had developed special fondness for the young Guru Gobind Singh, who, too, often came here to sit in the Queen's lap giving her immense delight and spiritual solace.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Gai Ghat
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Pahila Bara, commonly known as Gurdwara Gai Ghat, is a holy Gurdwara of Sikh religion. It is located in the city of Patna, Bihar, India and dedicated to Guru Nanak Dev.

topPlaces.name: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium metro station
topPlaces.description: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium is an upcoming underground metro station on the north–south corridor of the Blue Line of Patna Metro in Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium
topPlaces.description: Moin-ul-Haq Stadium previously known as Dr. Rajendra Prasad stadium is located in Rajendra Nagar, Patna, Bihar, India.

topPlaces.name: Rajendra Nagar, Patna
topPlaces.description: Rajendra Nagar (often spelled Rajender Nagar) is a neighbourhood in Patna, Bihar, India. It is named after Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India.

topPlaces.name: Prakash Punj
topPlaces.description: Prakash Punj or Bahu Uddeshiya Prakash Kendra and Udyan (Multi Purpose light centre and Garden) is under construction park at Guru Ka Bag near Bazzar samiti in Patna Sahib area.

topPlaces.name: Saidpura
topPlaces.description: Saidpura or Saidpur is a census town in the Indian state of Bihar. It is a part of Patna urban agglomeration.

items.note: About three kilometers east of Takht Sri Patna Sahib is where Guru Tegh Bahadur first alighted in a garden (bagh) belonging to Nawabs Rahim Bakhsh and Karim Bakhsh, nobles of Patna, and where the sangat of Patna along with the young Guru Gobind Singh came out to receive him back from his four-year-long odyssey. A shrine commemorative of the first meeting of Tegh Bahadur and Gobind Singh was established here.

Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat in a narrow lane close to Takht Sri Harmandir Sahib marks the house where King Fateh Chand Maini lived. His childless Queen had developed special fondness for the young Guru Gobind Singh, who, too, often came here to sit in the Queen's lap giving her immense delight and spiritual solace.
Gurdwara Pahila Bara, commonly known as Gurdwara Gai Ghat, is a holy Gurdwara of Sikh religion. It is located in the city of Patna, Bihar, India and dedicated to Guru Nanak Dev.

Rajendra Nagar (often spelled Rajender Nagar) is a neighbourhood in Patna, Bihar, India. It is named after Rajendra Prasad, the first President of India.
Prakash Punj or Bahu Uddeshiya Prakash Kendra and Udyan (Multi Purpose light centre and Garden) is under construction park at Guru Ka Bag near Bazzar samiti in Patna Sahib area.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Handi Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Handi Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Handi Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (8 km) and the nearest railway station is Patna Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Handi Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Handi Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Handi Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Handi Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Handi Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gurdwara Guru ka Bagh, Gurdwara Bal Lila Maini Sangat, Gurdwara Gai Ghat, Moin-ul-Haq Stadium metro station, Moin-ul-Haq Stadium.
